commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=29ed6c26fe343382dffbac6be9f7ef5d8305f96d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `openclaw doctor --explain`, adds check-scoped `doctor --fix --only` repair plumbing for focused health checks, documents the new doctor modes, and adds CLI/doctor tests.

PR surface: Source +472, Tests +359, Docs +54. Total +885 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks `doctor --explain` and keeps selector options tied to lint mode.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer product review plus contributor proof/fixes before any automation lane would be safe.

target.title: feat(doctor): add doctor --explain diagnostics [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `openclaw doctor --explain` for plain-English structured doctor findings.
- Shows what happened, why it matters, next steps, details, and focused repair availability per check.
- Adds explicit `doctor --fix --only <checkId>` focused repair for checks that opt into focused automatic repair.
- Keeps default `openclaw doctor` and `doctor --lint --json` behavior unchanged.

Closes #94241

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Adds plain-English explain output for structured doctor findings and exposes focused repair only for checks that explicitly support focused automatic repair.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, branch `codex/doctor-explain`, commit `29ed6c26`, Node `v23.6.1`, isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` temp directories.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```sh
  tmp=$(mktemp -d)
  mkdir -p "$tmp/home" "$tmp/state"
  printf '{}\n' > "$tmp/openclaw.json"
  HOME="$tmp/home" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmp/openclaw.json" pnpm openclaw doctor --explain --only core/doctor/gateway-config --non-interactive
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  doctor --explain: ran 1 check(s), 1 finding(s)

  Gateway config [warning]
    Check: core/doctor/gateway-config
    What happened: Gateway setup is incomplete.
    Why it matters: OpenClaw cannot reliably start or find the Gateway until this is configured.
    Try this: Run `openclaw configure` and set Gateway mode (local/remote), or `openclaw config set gateway.mode local`.
    Automatic repair: Not available for this check; use the suggested manual fix.
    Details:
    - gateway.mode: gateway.mode is unset; gateway start will be blocked.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: command exited `1` because the warning-level gateway-config finding was present, and the output rendered the new plain-English sections instead of JSON or lint rows.
- What was not tested: cross-platform/package install smoke, a real interactive TTY prompt confirmation, and the broad `pnpm check:changed` gate.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` was attempted locally but stopped before checks because the local Crabbox binary failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check. Interactive prompt behavior is covered by injected prompter tests rather than a live TTY recording.

Additional focused repair safety proof:

```sh
tmp=$(mktemp -d)
mkdir -p "$tmp/home" "$tmp/state"
printf '{}\n' > "$tmp/openclaw.json"
HOME="$tmp/home" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmp/openclaw.json" pnpm openclaw doctor --fix --only core/doctor/gateway-config
```

```text
Health check core/doctor/gateway-config does not support automatic repair.
[proof] exit=1
[proof] config={}
```

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/commands/doctor-explain.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main` (clean after fixes)
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes, additive CLI behavior behind `--explain` and `--fix --only`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Only focused repair writes for checks that explicitly opt into focused automatic repair; default doctor repair behavior is unchanged.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No new secret or network behavior. Existing `--allow-exec` gating is passed through to structured checks.

Highest-risk area: preserving lint/default doctor contracts while adding explain prompting. Mitigation: explicit mode-combination rejects, `--json` incompatibility checks, focused repair opt-in metadata, warning-to-nonzero behavior for selected repair, and autoreview-driven fixes.

## Current review state

AI-assisted PR. I understand the code changes and verified the behavior above. Opened as draft until CI/ClawSweeper feedback is visible.
